productDecision.decision: FLOW-22 CMS Publishing can proceed through the mobility/auth protocol after repairing the pre-existing service inheritance and tenant-context coupling defects.
notes: Functional state identifies FLOW-22 as designed with four service files and no topology-source portability test yet.
The current client route has reader, contributor, editor, platform oversight, and support fallback surfaces; role evidence still needs to be produced through the protocol phases.
The per-flow fix map names tenant context service decoupling as the first unblocking portability repair.

changes: Extended all four FLOW-22 services from MicroserviceBase with T633-T636 service descriptors.
Replaced direct ClsService and tenant-context key imports with TenantContextResolver.
Kept a legacy get(tenant) fallback so existing tests and harnesses remain compatible while production paths use the resolver.
Renamed injected database and queue members to dbFabric and queueFabric to avoid MicroserviceBase accessor collisions.
